seattle-tomorrow-forecast.content:
**Seattle Tomorrow (Friday, Feb 20)**

**Morning:** Cool and mostly cloudy, calm winds. Dry conditions expected. Low near 36°F.

**Afternoon:** Continued cloud cover with temperatures reaching 43°F. Light south winds around 5 mph developing.

**Evening:** Precipitation chances increase to 30%. Possible rain showers before 10pm, transitioning to a rain/snow mix overnight. Little to no snow accumulation.

*Bottom line: Carry an umbrella for the evening commute. Otherwise, a typical cool, gray February day.*

Overall, expect a cool, cloudy day with dry conditions until evening when precipitation chances increase.

orcas-island-tomorrow-forecast.content:
**🏝️ Orcas Island Forecast – Friday, Feb 20**

**Conditions:** Mostly cloudy, high 40°F, low 37°F. Light SE winds 3-8 mph.

**Ferry Travel:** ✅ Favorable – calm seas with 2 ft waves expected. Today's Small Craft Advisory lifts by 10 AM; smooth sailing tomorrow.

**Outdoor Activities:** Good conditions during daylight. Light winds ideal for hiking and exploring.

**Heads Up:** Rain/snow showers possible after 4 AM Saturday. Saturday brings gustier winds (up to 20 mph) and 40% precipitation chance—plan outdoor activities for Friday if possible.

wa-road-conditions.content:
## Washington State Road Conditions – Feb 19, 2026

**I-5 (Seattle Corridor)**
⚠️ DELAYS – Active Revive I-5 construction; lane closures and delays expected

**I-5 (Vancouver/Lakewood)**
⚠️ CAUTION – Multiple construction zones through 2026

**I-90 (Snoqualmie Pass)**
⚠️ CAUTION – Paving projects east of Vantage; check pass conditions for weather/chains

**SR-20 (North Cascades)**
🔴 CLOSED – Seasonal winter closure between Ross Dam and Rainy Pass

**SR-20 (Anacortes)**
✅ CLEAR – Open to ferry terminal

**Ferries**
⚠️ DELAYS – Kingston terminal retrofit causing 20-30 min delays on evening sailings; Colman Dock construction through April

**Alternate Routes**
- Seattle: Use transit or FlipYourTrip.org
- Mountain crossings: US-2 Stevens Pass or I-90 (SR-20 closed)

**Real-time updates:** wsdot.com/travel/real-time/map
wa-road-conditions.rawContent:
Based on my research, I have gathered available information from WSDOT sources. Here's the **Washington State Road Conditions Report** for February 19, 2026:

---

## 🚗 Washington State Road Conditions Summary

### **I-5 (Seattle Corridor)**
| Status | Details |
|--------|---------|
| ⚠️ **Active Construction** | **Revive I-5 Project** - Ongoing freeway closures and lane reductions in the Seattle area. Plan ahead and expect delays. |
| 🔧 **Construction** | I-5/179th Street Interchange improvements (2024-2031) in Vancouver area |
| 🔧 **Construction** | I-5 Gravelly-Thorne Connector project (Summer 2025 - Late Fall 2026) near Lakewood/Tillicum |
| 🔧 **Construction** | Olympic Region paving on I-5 sections (through November 2025) |

### **I-90 (Snoqualmie Pass)**
| Status | Details |
|--------|---------|
| 🔧 **Construction** | East of Vantage to Moses Lake paving project (July 2025-2026) - MP 138-148 and MP 175+ |
| ⛰️ **Mountain Pass** | Check real-time conditions at [wsdot.com/travel/real-time/mountainpasses](https://wsdot.com/travel/real-time/mountainpasses) for current weather, traction requirements, and chain advisories |

### **Highway 20 (North Cascades)**
| Status | Details |
|--------|---------|
| ❄️ **SEASONAL CLOSURE** | The North Cascades Highway (SR 20) is typically **CLOSED** during winter months between Ross Dam Trailhead and Rainy Pass. The highway usually reopens in late spring (typically April-May) depending on snowpack and avalanche conditions. |

### **SR-20 to Anacortes (Ferry Terminal)**
| Status | Details |
|--------|---------|
| ✅ **Open** | The western portion of SR-20 to Anacortes Ferry Terminal is open |
| ✅ **Completed** | Anacortes Terminal Tollbooth Replacement Project completed Winter 2024 |

---

## ⛴️ Washington State Ferries (Anacortes/San Juan Islands)

**Active Alerts (as of Feb 18, 2026):**

| Alert | Details |
|-------|---------|
| 🚢 **Sailing Canceled** | Feb 20 - 10:15 PM Bremerton to Seattle canceled for refueling |
| 💳 **New Fee** | Starting March 1, 2026 - 3% card payment fee on all transactions |
| 🔨 **Construction** | Colman Dock (Seattle) construction through early April |
| 🔨 **Kingston** | Terminal seismic retrofit through July 2026 - 20-30 min delays on 9:40 PM Kingston-Edmonds sailings (Tue-Thu) |
| 🚧 **SR 104 Kingston** | Weekday roadwork delays near Kingston terminal |

---

## 📍 Recommendations

1. **I-5 Seattle**: Use [FlipYourTrip.org](https://www.seattletraffic.org/) to find alternatives; consider transit during Revive I-5 closures
2. **I-90 Snoqualmie**: Check [wsdot.com](https://wsdot.com/travel/real-time/mountainpasses) for current pass conditions before traveling
3. **North Cascades (SR 20)**: Highway is closed for winter - use alternate routes (US-2 Stevens Pass or I-90)
4. **Anacortes Ferry**: SR-20 western segment is open; check [ferry schedules](https://wsdot.wa.gov/ferries/schedule/) and allow extra time

For real-time updates: **[wsdot.com/travel/real-time/map](https://wsdot.wa.gov/travel/real-time/map)** or download the **WSDOT mobile app**.

travel-summary.content:
**TRAVEL ADVISORY: Seattle → Orcas Island**
**Friday, February 20, 2026**

---

**Overall Recommendation: GOOD TRAVEL DAY** ✅

Tomorrow offers excellent conditions for your Seattle to Orcas Island trip. Calm seas, dry daytime weather, and light winds make this an ideal travel window.

**Key Conditions:**
- Ferry: Calm waters (2 ft waves), small craft advisory lifts by 10 AM
- Driving: SR-20 clear; expect I-5 construction delays (add 20-30 min)
- Orcas Island: Mostly cloudy, 40°F, light winds—great for outdoor activities

**Recommended Departure:** 7:00–8:00 AM from Seattle to beat construction traffic and catch mid-morning ferry sailings.

**Pack:** Rain jacket, layered clothing for 36-43°F temps, gloves/hat for wind chill. Book ferry reservations in advance and fill your tank before departure.

**Watch For:**
- I-5 construction delays—budget extra time
- Saturday conditions deteriorate (20 mph winds, 40% rain)—consider returning Friday evening if flexible

**Bottom Line:** Go tomorrow. Conditions are favorable across all segments of your journey. Early departure maximizes your day and avoids potential evening showers returning to Seattle.
travel-summary.rawContent:
# 🚗⛴️ Seattle → Orcas Island Travel Advisory
**Friday, February 20, 2026**

---

## 1️⃣ Current Conditions (Seattle)
| Metric | Value |
|--------|-------|
| Temperature | 35°F (feels like 28°F) |
| Sky | Partly Cloudy |
| Wind | 8 mph S |
| Today's Rain | 85% chance morning, clearing by evening |

---

## 2️⃣ Tomorrow's Travel Outlook

### 🚗 Driving: Seattle → Anacortes
| Segment | Status |
|---------|--------|
| I-5 Seattle Corridor | ⚠️ Construction delays expected |
| SR-20 to Anacortes | ✅ Clear and open |

**Weather en route:** Mostly cloudy, dry conditions through afternoon. 43°F high in Seattle area.

### ⛴️ Ferry: Anacortes → Orcas Island
| Factor | Conditions |
|--------|------------|
| Seas | ✅ Calm – 2 ft waves |
| Small Craft Advisory | Lifts by 10 AM today |
| Forecast | Favorable for all sailings |

### 🏝️ Orcas Island
Mostly cloudy, high 40°F. Light winds 3-8 mph. **Excellent conditions for outdoor activities.**

---

## 3️⃣ Recommended Departure Time

**🕗 Leave Seattle: 7:00–8:00 AM**

| Reason | Detail |
|--------|--------|
| Avoid I-5 delays | Beat peak construction traffic |
| Ferry timing | Target mid-morning sailing (calmer seas, advisory lifted) |
| Maximize daylight | Best outdoor conditions on Orcas |
| Beat evening weather | 30% rain chance in Seattle after 5 PM |

---

## 4️⃣ What to Pack/Prepare

### Essential
- ☔ **Umbrella/rain jacket** – evening showers possible
- 🧥 **Layered clothing** – temps 36-43°F
- 🧤 **Gloves/hat** – wind chill in upper 20s

### Travel Prep
- 🎫 **Book ferry reservations** – [wsdot.wa.gov/ferries](https://wsdot.wa.gov/ferries)
- ⛽ **Full tank** – limited services on Orcas
- 🍎 **Snacks/water** – for potential delays

---

## 5️⃣ Alerts & Concerns

| Priority | Alert |
|----------|-------|
| ⚠️ **Moderate** | I-5 construction causing delays – add 20-30 min buffer |
| ⚠️ **Moderate** | Colman Dock construction (Seattle ferry terminal) – not your route, but avoid if considering alternatives |
| 📋 **Info** | Rain/snow possible after 4 AM Saturday – plan return accordingly |
| 📋 **Info** | Saturday brings gustier winds (20 mph) and 40% rain – if flexible, return Friday evening |

---

## ✅ Bottom Line

**Tomorrow is an excellent day for this trip.** Calm ferry conditions, dry daytime weather, and light winds make Friday ideal. Leave early to avoid I-5 construction delays, and consider returning Friday evening if you want to avoid Saturday's deteriorating conditions.
